name: dbmate
description: Dbmate is a lightweight, framework-agnostic database migration tool written in Go. It supports PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, ClickHouse, and BigQuery with plain SQL migrations.

subcommands.name: new
subcommands.description: Create a new migration file with a timestamp prefix. The file contains empty -- migrate:up and -- migrate:down sections for writing SQL.

args.name: name
args.description: Name for the migration (used in the filename)

subcommands.name: up
subcommands.description: Apply all pending migrations in chronological order. Creates the database if it does not exist. Skips migrations that have already been applied.

options.name: --url
options.description: Database connection URL (overrides DATABASE_URL)

options.name: --env
options.description: Environment variable name containing the database URL (default: DATABASE_URL)

options.name: --migrations-dir
options.description: Path to the migrations directory

options.name: --migrations-table
options.description: Name of the migrations tracking table (default: schema_migrations)

options.name: --no-dump-schema
options.description: Skip updating the schema dump file after migration

options.name: --strict
options.description: Fail if migrations are applied out of order

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Show verbose output including SQL being executed

subcommands.name: down
subcommands.description: Roll back the most recently applied migration by executing its -- migrate:down section.

options.name: --url
options.description: Database connection URL

options.name: --env
options.description: Environment variable name for database URL

options.name: --migrations-dir
options.description: Path to migrations directory

options.name: --migrations-table
options.description: Name of the migrations tracking table

options.name: --no-dump-schema
options.description: Skip updating the schema dump file

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Show verbose output

subcommands.name: rollback
subcommands.description: Alias for the 'down' command. Roll back the most recently applied migration.

options.name: --url
options.description: Database connection URL

options.name: --env
options.description: Environment variable name for database URL

options.name: --migrations-dir
options.description: Path to migrations directory

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Show verbose output

subcommands.name: migrate
subcommands.description: Apply all pending migrations (alias for 'up'). Creates the database if it doesn't exist and applies migrations in order.

options.name: --url
options.description: Database connection URL

options.name: --env
options.description: Environment variable name for database URL

options.name: --migrations-dir
options.description: Path to migrations directory

options.name: --strict
options.description: Fail if migrations are out of order

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Show verbose output

subcommands.name: create
subcommands.description: Create the database specified in the connection URL if it does not already exist.

options.name: --url
options.description: Database connection URL

options.name: --env
options.description: Environment variable name for database URL

subcommands.name: drop
subcommands.description: Drop the database specified in the connection URL. WARNING: This permanently destroys the database and all data.

options.name: --url
options.description: Database connection URL

options.name: --env
options.description: Environment variable name for database URL

subcommands.name: dump
subcommands.description: Dump the current database schema to a file (default: db/schema.sql). Uses the database's native dump utility (pg_dump, mysqldump, etc.).

options.name: --url
options.description: Database connection URL

options.name: --env
options.description: Environment variable name for database URL

options.name: --schema-file
options.description: Path for the schema dump file (default: db/schema.sql)

subcommands.name: status
subcommands.description: Display the status of all migrations, showing which have been applied and which are pending.

options.name: --url
options.description: Database connection URL

options.name: --env
options.description: Environment variable name for database URL

options.name: --migrations-dir
options.description: Path to migrations directory

options.name: --migrations-table
options.description: Name of the migrations tracking table

subcommands.name: wait
subcommands.description: Wait for the database to become available. Useful in Docker/container environments where the database may take time to start.

options.name: --url
options.description: Database connection URL

options.name: --env
options.description: Environment variable name for database URL

options.name: --wait-timeout
options.description: Timeout in seconds to wait for database (default: 60s)

subcommands.name: load
subcommands.description: Load the schema file (db/schema.sql) directly into the database, bypassing individual migrations. Useful for initializing a new database from a known schema.

options.name: --url
options.description: Database connection URL

options.name: --env
options.description: Environment variable name for database URL

options.name: --schema-file
options.description: Path to the schema file to load

globalOptions.name: --env
globalOptions.description: Environment variable name containing the database URL

globalOptions.name: --migrations-table
globalOptions.description: Name of the table to track applied migrations (default: schema_migrations)

globalOptions.name: --schema-file
globalOptions.description: Path to the schema dump file (default: ./db/schema.sql)

globalOptions.name: --no-dump-schema
globalOptions.description: Do not update the schema dump file after migrating

globalOptions.name: --wait-timeout
globalOptions.description: Timeout in seconds for the wait command (default: 60)

globalOptions.name: --strict
globalOptions.description: Fail if migrations would be applied out of order

detectors.name: dbmate_database_url_detect
detectors.description: Check if DATABASE_URL environment variable is set
